name: Kitchen God
description: The Kitchen God, also known as Zao Jun (灶君) or Zao Shen (灶神), is one of the most important deities in Chinese folk religion. He resides in every household's kitchen, observing the family's behavior throughout the year. At the end of each year, he ascends to Heaven to report to the Jade Emperor (玉皇大帝) on the family's conduct, both good and bad. The Jade Emperor then decides whether to reward or punish the family based on the Kitchen God's report. To ensure a favorable report, families often offer the Kitchen God sweet treats, such as honey or sticky rice cakes, to 'sweeten his mouth' and encourage him to speak well of them. Some families even smear his lips with honey or molasses before he ascends to heaven. The Kitchen God is typically depicted as an elderly man with a long beard, often accompanied by his wife. He is a protector of the household and a guardian of the family's moral compass. He is also associated with fire and the hearth, representing the heart of the home. His image is usually a paper effigy pasted above or near the stove, which is replaced annually before the Chinese New Year. The importance of the Kitchen God highlights the significance of family harmony, moral behavior, and respect for tradition in Chinese culture. He serves as a constant reminder to families to live virtuously and maintain a good reputation, as their actions are always being observed and judged.
subtitle: Guardian of the Hearth and Home

keyMyths.title: Ascension to Heaven
keyMyths.description: The Kitchen God ascends to Heaven annually to report on the family's behavior to the Jade Emperor, influencing the family's fortune for the upcoming year.

keyMyths.title: Sweetening the Mouth
keyMyths.description: Families offer sweet treats to the Kitchen God before his ascent, hoping to 'sweeten his mouth' and encourage him to give a positive report.

keyMyths.title: The Kitchen God's Wife
keyMyths.description: The Kitchen God is often depicted with his wife, who assists him in observing the family's actions and maintaining household harmony.
